description: Phase 11 eval prompt set -- AI infrastructure technical content, bare topic format

prompts.prompt: Explain what arithmetic intensity is and why it determines whether a GPU kernel is memory-bandwidth bound or compute bound.
Describe the GPU memory hierarchy from registers to HBM and explain how latency and capacity trade off at each level.
Explain what the roofline model is and how you use it to decide what to optimize in a GPU kernel.
What is a CUDA warp and why does warp divergence hurt performance?
Explain the difference between shared memory and HBM on a GPU and why this distinction matters for kernel design.
What is memory coalescing in CUDA and what happens to bandwidth utilization when access patterns are not coalesced?
Explain SM occupancy -- what it measures, what limits it, and why maximizing it does not always improve performance.
Explain the tiling pattern in matrix multiplication -- what problem it solves and how it uses shared memory to reduce HBM round trips.
Why does naive attention have O(N squared) memory complexity and what specific algorithmic change does Flash Attention make to reduce this?
Explain the online softmax trick used in Flash Attention -- what it computes and why it allows tiling without materializing the full attention matrix.
What is kernel fusion and why does a fused softmax outperform an unfused PyTorch implementation on memory-bound workloads?
Explain what Triton is, how it differs from writing CUDA C directly, and what class of kernels it is best suited for.
Explain why INT4 quantization does not automatically produce 4x throughput over FP16 and what the Marlin kernel does differently to achieve near-theoretical speedup.
What is the difference between NF4 and standard INT4 quantization formats and why does NF4 produce better quality at the same bit width?
Explain Ring AllReduce step by step -- what each GPU sends, what it receives, and why bus bandwidth utilization approaches 100 percent as the ring size grows.
What is the difference between data parallelism, tensor parallelism, and pipeline parallelism in distributed training?
Explain ZeRO Stage 1, 2, and 3 -- what each stage shards and what memory savings each provides relative to standard DDP.
Why does NVLink bandwidth matter so much more than PCIe bandwidth for multi-GPU training, and at what cluster scale does interconnect become the binding constraint?
What is gradient checkpointing, what does it trade, and when is it the right choice versus increasing batch size?
Explain the KL penalty in RLHF training -- what it prevents and what happens to model behavior when it is set too low versus too high.
What is mixed precision training, why is the forward pass run in BF16 while the optimizer state is kept in FP32, and what failure mode does this prevent?
Explain the KV cache -- what it stores, why it grows with sequence length, and what GPU memory constraint it creates at 100K context.
What is PagedAttention and how does managing the KV cache like OS virtual memory increase effective batch size?
Explain the latency versus throughput tension in LLM inference serving and what workload characteristics determine which to optimize for.
What is speculative decoding, when does it produce speedup, and what property of the draft model determines the acceptance rate?
Explain continuous batching in vLLM -- why it improves GPU utilization over static batching and what the tradeoff is.
What is the prefill versus decode distinction in autoregressive generation and why do they have different hardware utilization profiles?
Explain AWQ quantization -- how it differs from naive weight quantization and why it preserves quality better at INT4 precision.
Explain RoPE -- what problem it solves compared to learned absolute position embeddings and how encoding position as a rotation enables context length extrapolation.
What is Grouped Query Attention, how does it differ from Multi-Head Attention, and what is the direct impact on KV cache size for a 70B model at 100K context?
Explain the Mixture of Experts architecture -- how routing works, what active parameter count means versus total parameter count, and what the load balancing problem is.
What is SwiGLU and why does it produce better loss than GELU at the same parameter count?
Explain RMSNorm versus LayerNorm -- what computation RMSNorm removes, why quality is preserved, and where the inference speedup comes from.
What do residual connections do in a transformer and why are they essential for training stability in deep networks?
Explain the Chinchilla scaling law -- what it says about the optimal ratio of model parameters to training tokens and what the practical implication is for training budget allocation.
What is the MLP block in a transformer doing mechanistically and what is the current understanding of what information it stores?
Explain the LoRA matrix decomposition -- what W plus BA means, why rank r determines the capacity of the update, and why this works for behavioral fine-tuning.
What is QLoRA and what specific combination of techniques allows fine-tuning a 70B model on a single A100 80GB?
What is catastrophic forgetting in fine-tuning and why does LoRA reduce it compared to full parameter fine-tuning?
Explain the Bradley-Terry preference model -- what it assumes about human preferences and how it produces a training loss for reward modeling.
Why did DPO largely replace PPO-based RLHF in practice -- what specific complexity and instability problems does DPO eliminate?
Explain what the DPO loss function is doing -- what it increases, what it decreases, and how the reference model enforces the KL constraint implicitly.
What is reward hacking in RLHF and why does it happen even when the reward model was trained on genuine human preferences?
Explain the ReAct pattern -- what explicit structure it adds to agent reasoning and why it reduces hallucination and wasted tool calls compared to unstructured generation.
What is RAG, what problem does it solve for LLMs with fixed training cutoffs, and what are the two components whose quality determines end-to-end RAG performance?
Explain cosine similarity for embedding retrieval -- why the direction of an embedding vector encodes meaning while magnitude is normalized away.
What is HyDE in RAG retrieval, what query-document mismatch problem does it address, and what is the tradeoff of generating a hypothetical document before searching?
Explain the four types of memory an agent has -- working, episodic, semantic, and procedural -- and what storage backend is appropriate for each.
What is prompt injection in agentic systems, why is it harder to defend against than SQL injection, and what is the defense-in-depth approach?
Explain why a dedicated vector database is the wrong default storage choice for a multi-agent system that needs both similarity search and transactional state updates.
